pages.text:
BLOG

Lubrication Solutions for Extreme Conditions in Steel Plants

6 min read | 20 April 2023

Lubrication is a crucial element required for any moving machine and its parts. Without lubricants, the life-expectancy and efficient functioning of any rotating equipment is greatly reduced. Productivity is delayed due to premature bearing failures, increased use of energy, and machine downtime. This leads to further losses of production which calls for new and improved lubrication solutions.

When lubricants are used in machines, their main functions are to:

Transport contaminated particles to filters and maintain cleanliness

Create a film between metal surfaces to reduce friction

Protect metal parts from corrosion

Cool surfaces by dissipating heat

The steel manufacturing industry involves processes that are exposed to high temperatures, extreme loads, and corrosive and humid environments. These factors influence the way lubricants work on machines, creating more demand for super lubricating abilities.

Molygraph’s speciality lubricants have, therefore, been developed to offerseveral benefits to clients.Our diverse and complete range of lubricant solutions helps in managing critical steel manufacturing processes. The cost of maintenance is reduced as productivity is improved through quality lubrication. Our products are environment-friendly, making them a safe and protective fit for operators.

Molygraph’s customised range of speciality lubricants

We have developed three versatile lubrication applications for the steel industry, namely:

Integrated Steel Plant

Steel Pelletizing Plant

Sponge Iron Plant

1. Integrated Steel Plant

Our lubrication products cover a wide range of areas at integrated steel plants. We aim to help reduce costs by enhancing productivityregardless of any extreme environmental conditions. From iron-making to finished products, lubrication solutions are, thus, developed to meet all requirements atIntegrated Steel Plants.

The application components shown below are based on the areas our products can be used.

Raw Material Preparations: Storage

Raw Material Processing: Coke Oven Plant, Lime Plant, Sinter Plant

Pig Iron Production: Blast Furnace

Caster: Continuous Caster

Hot Rolling Mill: Work/Back-Up Roll Bearings, Chokes Window Liner, Rolling Mill Drive Shaft Gear Couplings, and Re-Heating Furnace Table Roll Bearing

Cold Rolling Mill: Work/Back-Up Roll Bearings, Roll Chokes Liner, Rolling Mill Drive Shaft, Gear Couplings, Pickling Line, Squeezer Roll Bearing, Water Rinse Tank Bearing, Wringer Roll Bearing, Arb Blower Fan Bearing

Bar and Rod Mill: Reheating Furnace Withdrawal/Roll Table Bearing, Work/Back-Up Roll Bearing, Roll Chokes Liner, and Rolling Mill Drive Shaft Gear Couplings

2. Steel Pelletizing Plant

The process of pelletizing iron involves converting iron ore fines into uniformly sized iron pellets. These pellets are then charged directly into the blast furnace. Our lubricating range of products is designed to meet the challenging and demanding processes involved in theSteel Pelletizing Plant.

The application components involved in the following processes are:

Grinding Mill: Ball Mill Girth Gear, Ball Mill Girth Gear Pinion Bearing

Mixture Machine: Mixture Machine Bearing, Mixture Machine Gear

Balling Disc: Balling Disc Girth Gear, Balling Disc Bearing

Travelling Guide: TG Plumber Block Bearing

Kiln: Kiln Girth Gear – Spray System, Support Roller Bearing/Kiln, Thrust Bearing/Bucket, Elevator Bearing, Sliding Surface of Track

3. Sponge Iron Plant

Sponge iron is produced from the direct reduction of iron ore and it can be further processed into wrought iron.It is useful as feedstock for speciality steel manufacturers. The lubricants in this process are required to be thermally and mechanically stable. It should also be able to resist water washouts.

Our range of lubricants covers the entire spectrum of applications including Kiln & CoolerSlip Seal, Kiln & CoolerGirth Gear, Support RollerBearing, Vibrator ScreenBearing, Id/Fd Fan Bearing, and so much more. You can know more about theSponge Iron Planton our website.

Are you prepared to take your steel manufacturing processes to the next level? We have aField Engineering Services Teamready to support, guide you and provide end-to-end lubrication education. Get the best deals in lubrication for steel with Molygraph.

Share

pages.title: Can Lithium be replaced by Calcium in Greases? - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases
pages.text:
Can Lithium be replaced by Calcium in Greases? - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases

BLOG

Can Lithium be replaced by Calcium in Greases?

3 min read | 24 April 2023

Lithium grease is considered a multi-purpose grease. Due to its properties like good shear stability, excellent water resistance, high temperature capability and good response to various additives and performances, it dominates the worldwide market.

However, over the past few months, the price of lithium has increased drastically. So, this rising trend leads to a question: Is using lithium-based grease still viable in the long-term given the increase in price?

Suitable Alternatives:

Depending on the application and operating conditions, suitable soap thickeners can be selected as an alternative to conventional lithium and lithium complex greases.

Calcium Sulfonategrease shows a very high dropping point of 300°C compared to lithium grease. It shows exceptional extreme pressure and antiwear performance, excellent water washout, corrosion prevention, high temperature resistance, and good mechanical and shear stability. Their pumpability is slightly less as compared to Lithium greases. Calcium Sulfonate grease does not need any additives to meet certain requirements as compared to lithium grease.

Due to excellent properties, performance and economic viability, calcium sulfonate grease can replace lithium grease and work as a multi-purpose grease.

Calcium 12 hydroxy stearategrease is also considered as an alternative to lithium-based grease. Calcium portfolio in terms of environmental pollution is better than lithium. Calcium hydroxide is not a pollutant. Calcium grease shows better roll stability, excellent water washout and mechanical stability.

Lithium-Calciumgrease is another effective alternative for lithium grease. In this type of grease, lithium is partially replaced by calcium. Thus, most of the properties remain unaffected.

This means that manufacturers and customers can remain unaffected by the rising price of lithium by switching to Calcium – Based grease without compromising the performance of lithium-based grease.

Comparative Performance Data of lithium, calcium sulfonate, lithium-calcium and calcium 12-hydroxy stearate grease:

Base Grease

Lithium

Calcium Sulfonate

Lithium-Calcium

Molygraph calcium 12-hydroxy stearate grease

Roll Stability (ASTM D-1831,%)

Dropping Point (ASTM D-2265,°C)

213

300+

Water washout (ASTM D-1264,%)

12

1.5

8

Weld Load (ASTM D-2596,kgs)

Lithium grease is considered a multi-purpose grease. Due to its properties like good shear stability, excellent water resistance, high temperature capability and good response to various additives and performances, it dominates the worldwide market.

However, over the past few months, the price of lithium has increased drastically. So, this rising trend leads to a question: Is using lithium-based grease still viable in the long-term given the increase in price?

Suitable Alternatives:

Depending on the application and operating conditions, suitable soap thickeners can be selected as an alternative to conventional lithium and lithium complex greases.

Calcium Sulfonategrease shows a very high dropping point of 300°C compared to lithium grease. It shows exceptional extreme pressure and antiwear performance, excellent water washout, corrosion prevention, high temperature resistance, and good mechanical and shear stability. Their pumpability is slightly less as compared to Lithium greases. Calcium Sulfonate grease does not need any additives to meet certain requirements as compared to lithium grease.

Due to excellent properties, performance and economic viability, calcium sulfonate grease can replace lithium grease and work as a multi-purpose grease.

Calcium 12 hydroxy stearategrease is also considered as an alternative to lithium-based grease. Calcium portfolio in terms of environmental pollution is better than lithium. Calcium hydroxide is not a pollutant. Calcium grease shows better roll stability, excellent water washout and mechanical stability.

Lithium-Calciumgrease is another effective alternative for lithium grease. In this type of grease, lithium is partially replaced by calcium. Thus, most of the properties remain unaffected.

This means that manufacturers and customers can remain unaffected by the rising price of lithium by switching to Calcium – Based grease without compromising the performance of lithium-based grease.

pages.title: NSF Certified Lubricants For Food & Pharma Industry| Molygraph
pages.text:
NSF Certified Lubricants For Food & Pharma Industry| Molygraph

BLOG

Food for thought: Are NSF Certified lubricants necessary for the Food & Pharma industry?

3 min read | 20 April 2023

Food, water and edible consumer products are a daily necessity. Since these products are for consumption, it is essential to ensure that the manufacturing process of these products is 100% safe. The Food and Pharma industry is perhaps one of the top segments that require strict adherence to rules and stipulations. One cannot risk contamination.

How do we ensure that the food & pharma products that we use are safe for consumption?

The NSF International was founded in 1944 by the University of Michigan’s School of Public Health as theNational Sanitation Foundation(NSF) to standardize sanitation and food safety requirements. NSF certification is your key to make sure that the products you use meet strict standards for public health protection.

Machinery used in Food and Pharma manufacturing requires regular maintenance. Using the right lubricants can drastically increase life and reduce maintenance downtime. However, incidental contact with food is something the maintenance managers have to look at closely. To this effect, NSF certified lubricants become necessary.

In the case of lubricants, NSF has three levels of certifications: H1, H2 and H3.

NSF-H1(Highest level of certification):These lubricants are safe in case of incidental food contact on production lines. Which is crucial since specialty greases, synthetic gear oils, and blended lubricating oils form a core part of our Food and Pharma product line.

They are used in machinery such as pumps, conveyor belts, mixers, etc. The applications include canning, blending, cutting, slicing, bottling, and brewing, among many others.

Check out Molygraph’s range of NSF H1-certified lubricantshere.

NSF H2:These lubricants are food safe, but are not supposed to come in contact with food. Hence, they are used in machines that do not come in direct contact with food, like the forklift.

NSF H3:These are additives used in the formulation of food-grade products. These are usually edible oils like sunflower oil, cottonseed oil, etc.

Molygraph’s SAFOL Rangeis one of the best collections of NSF and Halal certified Food & Pharma Lubricants available in the market, like:

LUBRICATING, GEAR & CONVEYOR CHAIN OILS and FOOD GRADE GREASES

Check our range online.

CHOOSE OPTIMUM PERFORMANCE WITH MOLYGRAPH.

pages.title: Molygraph || Understanding The Purpose of ASTM tests for corrosion resistance of lubricating greases - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases
pages.text:
Molygraph || Understanding The Purpose of ASTM tests for corrosion resistance of lubricating greases - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases

BLOG

Understanding the purpose of ASTM tests for corrosion resistance of lubricating greases

4 min read | 13 December 2022

ASTM standard tests are widely recognized as industry benchmarks that validate the effectiveness of lubricating greases in safeguarding machinery under demanding conditions. These tests are essential for selecting the right greases that function efficiently as rust and corrosion protectors.

The Importance of ASTM Tests in Lubricant Selection

With a multitude of greases available on the market, each offering different lubrication properties, choosing the right grease is vital to keep machine components and bearings operating smoothly. Industrial machinery often faces extreme conditions such as high temperatures, rapid speeds, and heavy loads, which can accelerate wear and tear. Additionally, environmental factors like exposure to water, corrosive chemicals, fumes, and high humidity further challenge the durability of machinery components.

ASTM standard tests help manufacturers and users evaluate the performance of lubricating greases, ensuring they provide the necessary protection against corrosion while maintaining lubrication efficiency. At Molygraph, we have developed our own stringent testing methods based on ASTM standards to assess the performance of our greases in real-world applications.

Molygraph’s Rigorous ASTM-Based Testing Methods

To guarantee that our greases offer superior corrosion protection and performance, Molygraph employs the following ASTM tests:

1. ASTM D6138 - EMCOR Rust Test

This test evaluates the rust resistance of lubricated bearings under simulated operational conditions. Cleaned and lubricated bearings are partially submerged in water and subjected to a speed of 83 ± 5 rpm, alternating between running and stopping for one week. After the test period, the bearings are cleaned, examined, and rated based on the extent of corrosion observed.

2. ASTM D1743 - Corrosion Preventive Test

In this test, lubricated bearings are run under a thrust load for 60 ± 3 seconds to distribute the lubricant uniformly. Water is then introduced, and the bearings are stored for 48 ± 0.5 hours at 52 ± 1°C with 100% relative humidity. Post-storage, the bearings are inspected for signs of corrosion, ensuring the lubricant’s effectiveness in preventing rust.

3. ASTM D4048 - Copper Corrosion Test

This test assesses the chemical resistance of lubricating greases. A copper strip is immersed in a grease sample and heated at 100 ± 1°C for 24 hours. After the heating period, the strip is washed and compared against standard charts to determine the level of copper corrosion, providing insights into the grease’s protective capabilities.

The purpose behind these tests is to ensure that we formulate the high-performing greases that offer the best corrosion protection in the harshest environments.

BLOG

Molygraph: A One-Stop Solution for High-Performance Lubricants

4 min read | 24 April 2023

Since its inception, Molygraph Lubricants has been focused on creating lubricant formulations that are a class apart. In the last 50 years, our expertise in working with various base oils, chemistries, and top-notch additives has helped us carve our mark among Industrial Lubricant manufacturers.

A Brief Note on Molygraph

The Molygraph family is proud to have a team of exceptionally proficient and experienced professionals who put in substantial effort to deliver high-quality lubricants at a competitive cost. The primary aim is to enhance productivity and efficiency through sustainable technological innovations. Starting from formulation to the final launch, thorough checks are constantly carried out to ensure optimum results.

Industries Served By Molygraph

With a core focus on specialty lubricants, Molygraph has developed market and application-specific products through the years and established a stronghold in a wide range of industrial domains, namelyMRO,Food & Beverage,Metal Cutting & Forming,Punching & Stamping,Mining,Steel,Petrochemicals,andAutomotive.

Products Offered By Molygraph

Multiple industries require us to create a versatile range of products to fulfil all the lubrication demands. Molygraph’s products range from basic greases to specially customized lubricants custom-made for targeted applications. Here is an overview of our range:

Lubricating greases and Anti-seize formulated with various chemistries like Lithium, Aluminium Grease Chemistry, Lithium Complex, Polyurea, Calcium Sulphonate, these products help bearings run not only at high temperatures and heavy loads but also protect them against wear, rust, and corrosion. They are the best options for general maintenance as well as heavy-duty applications in the Steel and Mining industry.

Lubricating oils likeGear OilsandConveyor Chain Oilsformulated with paraffinic oils, Polyalphaolefins, Esters, and Polyglycols help reduce friction, heat and wear and prevent metal surfaces from rust, corrosion, and contamination.

Graphol range of forging lubricants formulated as either a dispersion of graphite in water and oil or synthetic polymers in water. These are extremely effective for a clean release with an excellent surface finish for forged components.

Smoothkut, Smoothform, and a range of punching oils, also known as vanishing oils, can effectively contribute to improvements in production capacities. This entire range also meets wetting properties, material compatibility, refrigerant compatibility, and is used in the manufacturing of HVAC units, Aluminum food containers, and motor cores.

Food-grade lubricants are essential for any food processing unit, and Molygraph’sSafol Rangeof NSF and Halal-Certified food-grade greases and gear oils are Non-toxic and specially formulated with best-in-class additives.

Cleaners & Degreasersare essential in the maintenance of all machinery, and to serve this purpose, our range of water and solvent-based products is extremely effective, whether it be light-duty or heavy-duty applications.

pages.title: Polyurea Greases – Applications and Properties | Molygraph
pages.text:
Polyurea Greases – Applications and Properties | Molygraph

BLOG

Polyurea Greases – Applications and Properties

10 min read | 20 April 2023

Buying a lubricant is tricky and engineers work hard to find world-class lubricants that suits their equipment for high-performance.

Molygraphformulates specialty lubricants to meet high expectations of the Customers and supplies wide range of lubricant solutions for industrial, electrical, and mechanical maintenance.

Molygraph’s Polyurea greasetechnology provides ample opportunities to meet the lubrication requirements of tomorrow’s technologies. The invention and development of Polyurea grease is the most important innovation in grease technology in the last 70 years. The major advancement in this technology happened in Japan.

Today Polyurea grease represents more than 50% of the Japanese grease market. Looking at the inherent properties of Polyurea grease and the benefits that they offer, the grease technology has continued to advance and is now robust enough to be used in a variety of applications, replacing traditional grease chemistries.

What’s Polyurea Grease?

Polyurea Grease is a premium first-class multipurpose, high temperature, extreme pressure grease. It is specially formulated to offer the extreme lubrication requirements of long service applications such as electric powered motor bearing.

Polyurea Thickenerswere ﬁrst introduced in 1954. Since then, many efforts were made to broaden a ﬁbrous polyurea grease, ordinarily as a replacement for barium greases, which have health, protection and environmental issues related to the toxicity of barium compounds. Many styles of thickeners are used in any ordinary grease.

The most popular greases are primarily based on lithium, lithium complex, and calcium or aluminium complex cleaning soap thickeners. In recent years, polyurea thickened greases were gaining in significance, especially in metallic mill non-stop castors, automotive wheel bearings, regular speed joints, electric instrumentation and auxiliary system bearings.

But, how are they made?

In the ﬁrst step, between 9-20 percent of weight of the selected isocyanates and amines and about forty percent of weight of base oils are loaded into an appropriate reaction kettle. The blend is then slowly blended at temperatures starting from 21 degree celsius to 204 degree celsius for lengthy sufficient motive formation of the thickener.They are synthesized in a three-step method involving a polymerization response of different isocyanates and amines in either mineral or synthetic base oils.

In the second step, suitable additives are mixed to the thickener. Portions of the final base oils are delivered to the thickener to achieve the desired viscosity grade and take it to temperatures appropriate for the addition of other overall performance additives. Those additives encompass antioxidants, extreme stress, antiwear agents and dyes.

Within the ﬁnal step, the grease is homogenized to reap the desired NLGI grade. Homogenizers are desired over colloids considering the fact that they generate polyurea greases with better texture and can signiﬁcantly modify the NLGI grade of the grease.

What will you get?

Polyurea greases offer excellent performance in industrial equipment. This is due to following capabilities:

Outstanding rust and corrosion protection.

Fantastic put on and intense stress safety.

An excessive load-wearing capacity.

Low noise.

Long bearing life

Outstanding thermal and oxidation stability.

Excellent high temperature performance.

Superb resistance to water washout and water spray-off.

Accelerated sturdiness and shear stability

Molygraph’s PUG Seriesof products can be used in a diverse range of applications and provide superior long life stability, corrosion protection, and allow excellent pumpability when using centralized lubrication systems.

Molygraph’sPUG series of greases are an extremely robust, long lasting and versatile choice for long life applications

You can use Polyurea greases in equipment like…

The output shaft of theconstant velocity (CV) jointshas the same rotational speed as that of the input shaft. It requires supreme quality greases to enhance the performance by protecting the inner additives from high stress. Polyurea greases offer superior outcomes and overall performance for CV Joints.

Polyurea greases are highly used insteel mill continuous castors(CC) The bearings of the rolling mills that carry the slab in CC have to support high weight and high temperature. High viscosity base oil greases are needed for such bearings to prevent rust and contamination. The lubrication in continuous castors is done by the centralized grease distribution system. This keeps the system lubricated at all times as fresh grease is pumped at frequent intervals. Polyurea greases offer excellent pumpability and act as an excellent sealing barrier to prevent contamination and corrosion. It can work in high temperatures and increase the lifetime of the bearing in CC.

Electric powered motors have double sealed bearings, and normally they do not require regreasing. However, some bearings being open require it to be re-lubricated periodically to replace grease that has deteriorated or leaked away. Polyurea grease has become the maximum endorsed grease in electric powered motors because they can

Lubricate at high temperatures

lessen friction

protect bearings against corrosion

Act as a seal to save you dirt and different contaminants

Many bearings require periodic renovation to prevent untimely failure, but many others require little maintenance like sealed bearings. Those incorporate seals to preserve the dirt out and do not require re-lubrication. Based on these necessities, lubrication engineers advocate a Polyurea grease, due to its outstanding lubrication residences in a huge temperature range. It also offers extraordinarily long life with sealed bearing designs.

While deciding on the perfect grease for your bearings that are exposed to temperatures above 120°C, polyurea thickened greases are top-rated.

Polyurea Grease is also recommended for…

Electric powered vehicles, excessive-temperature pump bearings and antifriction bearings running at high speeds, car wheel bearings, chassis elements and underhood systems, air conditioner compressor, snatch bearings, water pumps and pulleys.

What to expect from polyurea greases…

Polyurea greases provide load-supporting capability without degrading the thermal stability of the grease at high temperatures.

These greases exhibit a median life of about three to ﬁve times better than the lithium based greases.

Polyurea greases have a tendency to have very good water resistance, top mechanical stability and might exhibit high-shear balance.

Also, think about the general rules of lubrication

Molygraph’s grease has the advantage over other greases that it is effortlessly retained within the bearing arrangement, mainly where shafts are inclined or vertical. It additionally contributes to stopping the association to contaminants and moisture.

When choosing a grease, consider these most crucial factors:

Consistency, working temperature, viscosity of the base oil,

Rust inhibiting properties and the load supporting potential

But at the end of the day if you trust your instinct, you will find that…

Molygraph’s specialty lubricants can reduce your maintenance costs successfully. High-class Molygraph’s lubricants can play an important role in your company by mitigating the effects of wear and tear of the equipment.

Get in touch

We help you pick the right lubricant for your needs.

Share

Those incorporate seals to preserve the dirt out and do not require re-lubrication. Based on these necessities, lubrication engineers advocate a Polyurea grease, due to its outstanding lubrication residences in a huge temperature range. It also offers extraordinarily long life with sealed bearing designs.

While deciding on the perfect grease for your bearings that are exposed to temperatures above 120°C, polyurea thickened greases are top-rated.

Polyurea Grease is also recommended for…

Electric powered vehicles, excessive-temperature pump bearings and antifriction bearings running at high speeds, car wheel bearings, chassis elements and underhood systems, air conditioner compressor, snatch bearings, water pumps and pulleys.

What to expect from polyurea greases…

Polyurea greases provide load-supporting capability without degrading the thermal stability of the grease at high temperatures.

These greases exhibit a median life of about three to ﬁve times better than the lithium based greases.

Polyurea greases have a tendency to have very good water resistance, top mechanical stability and might exhibit high-shear balance.

Also, think about the general rules of lubrication

Molygraph’s grease has the advantage over other greases that it is effortlessly retained within the bearing arrangement, mainly where shafts are inclined or vertical. It additionally contributes to stopping the association to contaminants and moisture.

When choosing a grease, consider these most crucial factors:

Consistency, working temperature, viscosity of the base oil,

Rust inhibiting properties and the load supporting potential

But at the end of the day if you trust your instinct, you will find that…

Molygraph’s specialty lubricants can reduce your maintenance costs successfully. High-class Molygraph’s lubricants can play an important role in your company by mitigating the effects of wear and tear of the equipment.

pages.title: Choosing The Right Thickener For Multifunctional Grease - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases | Molygraph
pages.text:
Choosing The Right Thickener For Multifunctional Grease - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases | Molygraph

BLOG

Choosing The Right Thickener For Multifunctional Grease

4 min read | 19 April 2023

When it comes to industrial lubrication, grease composition plays a critical role in ensuring optimal performance and durability. Greases consist of three main components: base oil (70-95%), thickener (3-30%), and additives (up to 10%). Among these, the thickener acts as the "sponge" that retains the base oil and additives, giving grease its semi-solid structure.

Greases are primarily classified based on their thickeners, which fall into two categories:

Soap-based thickeners (e.g., Lithium, Calcium)

Non-soap-based thickeners (e.g., Clay, Polyurea)

The majority of greases worldwide rely on soap-based thickeners due to their proven performance across a variety of applications.

Comparing Thickeners for Multifunctional Grease

Through extensive research and field tests, three common soap thickeners—Lithium, Lithium Complex, and Calcium Sulfonate—were evaluated based on key performance parameters:

Stability

High-temperature resistance

Water resistance

Extreme pressure performance

An ideal thickener must excel under these conditions to ensure long-lasting performance in demanding industrial environments.

Why Calcium Sulfonate Outshines Other Thickeners

Calcium Sulfonate grease has consistently outperformed both Lithium and Lithium Complex greases in critical applications. Here's why:

1. Superior Performance Without Additives

Unlike other thickeners, Calcium Sulfonate grease doesn't rely on performance additives to achieve high standards. It naturally provides excellent shear stability, mechanical stability, and anti-wear properties, ensuring minimal leakage and extended equipment life.

2. Exceptional Water Resistance

Calcium Sulfonate greases are highly resistant to water washouts, making them ideal for wet or humid environments. They maintain their integrity even when exposed to moisture, outperforming their Lithium-based counterparts.

3. High-Temperature Capability

With an impressive dropping point and resistance to heat, Calcium Sulfonate greases are built for high-temperature applications, ensuring consistent performance in extreme conditions.

4. Rust and Corrosion Protection

Calcium Sulfonate greases are derived from renowned rust inhibitors. This gives them unmatched corrosion resistance, ensuring durability and protection in challenging environments.

5. Versatility Across Industries

Thanks to their resistance to dust, dirt, and humidity, Calcium Sulfonate greases are widely used in demanding industries such as:

Construction and Mining

Marine and Transport

Steel and Power

Agriculture, Food Processing, and Drilling

A Cost-Effective Choice

Over the years, the price gap between Calcium Sulfonate and Lithium greases has narrowed significantly. Today, industry professionals see calcium sulfonate grease as a value-for-money solution.

Why Choose Molygraph?

At Molygraph, we pride ourselves on offering top-quality Calcium Sulfonate greases that deliver unmatched performance across diverse industries. Our products are the result of rigorous R&D and extensive field testing, ensuring reliability and efficiency in every application.

Ready to Upgrade?

Switch to Molygraph’s Calcium Sulfonate greases and experience the difference.

📞 Call us at +91 81044 09925

📧 Email us at sales@molygraph.com

pages.title: Oil and Gas Speciality Lubricants to meet the World’s Energy Demands - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases
pages.text:
Oil and Gas Speciality Lubricants to meet the World’s Energy Demands - Know about Lubricants, Oils and Greases

BLOG

Oil and Gas Speciality Lubricants to meet the World’s Energy Demands

5 min read | 20 April 2023

Several industries are re-inventing ways to use energy for different but specific purposes. For that matter, the demand for oil and gas as a source of energy is increasing daily. This has resulted in excessive use of drilling machines in extraction processes.

The drilling equipment, be it onshore or offshore, is exposed to extremely harsh and humid conditions. For the powerful and smooth functioning of exploration equipment, the lubricant products have to perform equally well. Its formulation must contain properties that can support equipment functioning under high pressure, wide temperature ranges, long operating hours, and should be H2S and hydrocarbon resistant.

In light of this, Molygraph has developed a series of speciality lubricants, for the following processes:

Threaded connections (Casing, Tubing, Rotary Connections, Drill Stem)– Thread Compounds

Valve lubrication and sealing– Valve Lubricants

Bearings– Multifunctional Greases

Wire ropes– Chain and Wire rope greases

Enclosed Gearboxes– Gear oils

Thread compounds

TheThread Compoundsdeveloped are made up of production and drilling compounds. These products come with a combination of solid lubricants having excellent anti-seize and load-bearing features. They help lubricate the threaded connections, nuts, and bolts under high pressure and temperature. This helps prevent the parts from galling, distorting, breaking, rusting, and fretting corrosion.

Valve lubricant

Our VLG series ofValve Lubricantshave been made from special thickener systems, solid lubricants, and synthetic base oils. Their main purpose is maintaining valve integrity. They contain preventive additives for extreme pressure, wear, and corrosion from H2S. This series helps control the flow, prevents leakage, and protects itself from degradation over time. As valves and fittings operate under extreme conditions, lubrication is required to support their performance.

Chain and wire rope grease

Wire ropes are also required to perform various activities over the rig. Therefore, its upkeep for a long service life with low maintenance is crucial. That is why Molygraph has developedWire Rope Lubricantsthat penetrate the core of strands that help reduce friction between strands and rope bends. The grease is highly resistant to water and emulsification. Therefore, this prevents strand surfaces from rusting and corroding in the presence of harsh environments.

Gear oils

Molygraph understands the uniqueGear Oil Requirementsfor different chemistries and viscosities. Therefore, we have developed special formulations to lubricate gearboxes. This helps the gearboxes to perform under conditions such as extreme heat, and heavy loads. It also protects from environments contaminated with dirt, debris, and water.

Multifunctional greases

There are many other conditions faced by industrial applications. They include bearings and sliding surfaces exposed to wide temperature ranges, high loads, water ingress, high humidity, etc. Our range ofMultifunctional Greasesis developed to tolerate these extreme conditions.

Cleaners and degreasers

We have developed eco-friendly cleaners and degreasers meant for 100% clean-up. Our range of solvent andWater-Based Cleaners & Degreasersmeet a variety of applications. Parts such as electromechanical assemblies, dies, moulds, bearings, and various other components are effectively cleaned and degreased.

At Molygraph, we strive to meet our customer’s demands and become the solution-providers to their problems. Our engineering experts would love to hear from you.

Get in touchwith us to know more about our range of drilling lubricants.

Share

pages.text:
BLOG

Electric Vehicles and NVH - Less is….more?

3 min read | 04 October 2023

In electric vehicles (EVs), minimizing Noise, Vibration, and Harshness (NVH) is essential. The absence of an internal combustion engine makes other sounds within the vehicle more prominent, which can impact passenger comfort. Enter damping greases—a critical solution for reducing NVH in various EV components. Here’s how these specialized lubricants make EVs quieter, smoother, and more enjoyable to drive:

Electric Motor Bearings

Application: Damping greases are applied to electric motor bearings to reduce noise and vibration, ensuring seamless operation.

Benefit: Delivers a quieter and smoother driving experience by addressing operational noise unique to electric motors.

Gearboxes and Transmissions

Application: Damping greases in gearboxes and transmission components minimize noise during power transfer and gear shifts.

Benefit: Reduces drivetrain noise, creating a more peaceful cabin environment that complements the quiet nature of EVs.

Switches and Controls

Application: Applied to buttons, switches, and rotary controls, damping greases reduce mechanical noise and enhance tactile feedback.

Benefit: Elevates the user interface experience, making every interaction with vehicle controls feel refined and premium.

Door Locks and Latches

Application: Used in door locks, latches, and hinges to prevent squeaking and enable smooth operation.

Benefit: Enhances cabin comfort and user satisfaction by ensuring quiet, seamless door functionality.

Cabin Adjustment Mechanisms

Application: Damping greases are used in seat adjusters, ventilation louvers, and other adjustment mechanisms for silent and smooth operation.

Benefit: Improves the perceived quality of the interior, delivering a premium experience for passengers.

Connector Plugs

Application: On connector plugs, damping greases enable smooth insertion and removal while protecting against wear and corrosion.

Benefit: Ensures durability and reliable electrical connections, which are critical for EV performance.

Why Damping Greases Are Vital for EVs

With the unique challenges presented by EVs, such as heightened NVH sensitivity, damping greases play an indispensable role. By enhancing comfort, reliability, and the overall user experience, they make EVs more appealing to modern drivers.

Discover how specialized damping greases can revolutionize your EV design—delivering a quieter, smoother, and more premium driving experience.

Ready to Learn More?

Contact us today to explore solutions customized for your EV applications!

pages.text:
BLOG

A brief history of Polyurea Greases and their application in Continuous Casters

6 min read | 12 Jan 2024

A brief history of Polyurea Greases and their application in Continuous Casters

Polyurea greases have been a significant component in the lubrication industry due to their superior performance characteristics under high temperatures and loads. Here is a brief overview of the history and development of polyurea greases:

1. Initial Development:

Polyurea greases were developed around the 1950s. They were formulated as an alternative to the then commonly used soap-thickened greases, like lithium, calcium, and sodium-based greases, which had limitations regarding stability, temperature resistance, and water resistance.

2. Structural Innovation:

The unique diurea thickener structure in polyurea greases is formed by the reaction between a diamine and a diisocyanate. This distinctive structure contributes to the grease's excellent mechanical and thermal stability, high dropping points, and good oxidative stability, setting polyurea greases apart from traditional soap-thickened greases.

3. Enhancement and Specialization:

Over the years, advancements in technology and a better understanding of polyurea chemistry have led to the development of more specialized and enhanced polyurea greases, catering to specific industrial needs. Modifications in base oils, additives, and thickener structures have enabled the formulation of polyurea greases with improved performance under extreme conditions and challenging environments.

4. Current Applications:

Today, polyurea greases are ubiquitous and are utilized in diverse applications including, but not limited to, electric motors, fans, pumps, automotive components, and various industrial machinery, where they continue to provide reliable lubrication, protection against wear, and extended service life of components.

Polyurea greases are widely used in continuous casters within the steel industry due to their distinctive properties that make them suitable for the extreme conditions and specific requirements of continuous casting processes. Here’s why polyurea greases are preferred in continuous casters:

1. High-Temperature Stability:

Continuous casters operate at high temperatures, and polyurea greases maintain their structural integrity and lubrication efficiency under such conditions, preventing premature breakdown and ensuring consistent lubrication.

2. Excellent Mechanical Stability:

Polyurea greases exhibit excellent shear stability, maintaining their consistency and staying in place under high shear conditions, which is crucial for the reliability of components such as bearings in continuous casters.

3. Good Water Resistance:

The presence of water and steam is common in continuous casters, and polyurea greases resist water washout effectively, maintaining a protective lubrication film on the components and preventing corrosion.

4. Long Service Life:

Polyurea greases offer extended service life due to their exceptional stability, reducing the frequency of re-lubrication intervals and minimizing maintenance requirements and downtime.

5. Resistance to Contaminants:

They resist contamination from mill scale and other debris, maintaining their lubricating properties and protecting equipment surfaces from wear and corrosion.

6. Oxidative Stability:

Polyurea greases exhibit resistance to oxidation, preventing the formation of harmful by-products and deposits, which is particularly important for maintaining component integrity and preventing premature failures.

7. Enhanced Load-Carrying Capacity:

The excellent load-carrying capacity of polyurea greases ensures protection against wear and damage under the heavy loads typical of continuous casting operations.

8. Broad Operating Temperature Range:

Polyurea greases can operate effectively over a wide temperature range, making them suitable for various sections of continuous casters, from high-temperature zones to cooler areas.

9. Compatibility with Various Base Oils:

The ability to formulate polyurea greases with various base oils allows for tailoring the lubricant’s properties to meet specific requirements and conditions of continuous casting processes.

Polyurea greases are chosen for continuous casters in the steel industry predominantly due to their ability to perform reliably under the extreme conditions of high temperatures, heavy loads, and contamination. Their superior properties such as high-temperature stability, mechanical stability, water resistance, and long service life make them invaluable for ensuring the smooth and efficient operation of continuous casters, reducing maintenance needs, and prolonging the life of components.

Share

pages.text:
BLOG

High-Performance Grease Solutions for Animal Feed Equipment

5 min read | 20 April 2023

As global demand for animal feed continues to rise, the machinery that powers feed production must perform at peak efficiency. According to a report by Data Bridge Market Research, the animal feed market is projected to grow at aCAGR of 3.4% between 2020 and 2027. This surge places significant pressure on feed manufacturing equipment like pellet mills, and hammer mills to operate continuously—often under extreme conditions.

To meet production targets and minimize maintenance costs,regular lubrication and equipment inspectionbecome essential. Poor lubrication is one of the leading causes of mechanical failure in animal feed machinery, leading toincreased downtime, component wear, and unplanned shutdowns.

Why Proper Greasing is Critical

It’s not just about applying grease—it’s about using the right grease, in the right way, at the right time. Feed equipment features many moving parts that operate under high loads and vibrations. These components rely on a strong film of oil or grease to reduce metal-to-metal contact, friction, and overheating.

Inadequate or poor-quality lubricationcan result in:

Premature bearing failure

Increased equipment wear

Elevated temperatures and vibrations

Excessive grease consumption

The Molygraph Advantage

At Molygraph, we’ve developedadvanced grease formulationstailored specifically for the demands of animal feed equipment. Our greases are fortified withextreme pressure (EP) additivesand aspecialized thickener system, delivering superior performance even under challenging operating conditions.

Our core focus is addressing lubrication challenges in:

Pellet Mill

Hammer Mill

Explore two of our most trusted solutions:

EP Greases for Hammer Mill

Our grease for hammer mills is engineered to handle extreme pressure and heavy-duty conditions:

Exceptional load-carrying capacity

High-temperature resistance

Water-resistant formulaprevents washout

Reduces grease consumption through optimal re-greasing cycles

Enables lower operating temperatures in bearings vs. conventional lubricants

Ensureslonger component life and zero unexpected downtime

Withstands shock loads and mechanical stress

EP Greases for Pellet Mill

Specially formulated for high-load applications in pelletizing equipment:

Prevents bearing failure due tolubrication gaps

BLOG

How Advanced Damping Lubricants Are Shaping the Future of In-Cabin Experience

4 min read | 30 August 2025

When you step into a modern electric vehicle, you notice something almost instantly: Thesilence.

Without the constant hum of an engine, the cabin becomes a quiet stage where every sound, click, and movement is amplified. While this acoustic clarity makes conversations easier and the ride more relaxing, it also brings a new challenge for automakers:how to make every movement inside the vehiclefeelandsoundrefined.

At Molygraph, we believe this is where specialty damping lubricants are transforming the game.

From Noise Reduction to Motion Design

In the past, lubricants in automotive interiors were selected primarily for their ability to reduce friction and wear. Today, they have a new role,curating the sensory experienceof motion inside a vehicle.

A well-formulated damping grease doesn’t just stop squeaks and rattles. It:

Creates a smooth, controlled resistance for tactile feedback.

Absorbs vibrations before they reach the cabin.

Eliminates micro-oscillations that can cause unwanted noise.

Whether it’s a gear selector, a rotary dial, or a sliding console,motion now has to be deliberate, quiet, and premium—every single time.

Engineering Precision for Every Interface

Vehicle interiors bring together a mix of materials: plastic, metal, and elastomers, each with its own behavior under stress, temperature, and motion. OurAutoDamp™ seriesis designed to master these interactions:

Metal–Metal: Damping resonance to reduce chatter.

Plastic–Metal: Suppressing stick-slip for a smoother glide.

Plastic–Plastic: Preventing squeaks while maintaining tactile elegance.

By tailoring base oils, thickeners, and additive packages, we ensure consistent performance from freezing mornings to scorching afternoons.

Why This Matters for OEMs and Tier-1 Suppliers

Brand Identity: Motion and sound now play a role in reinforcing a premium brand feel.

Durability: Our lubricants maintain stability across extreme temperatures and repeated use.

Custom Solutions: Each grease is engineered to meet OEM-specific validation criteria and performance benchmarks.

From HVAC units to seat systems, steering switches to HMI modules,we help ensure that every touchpoint in the cabin feels as refined as the vehicle’s engineering promises.

The Road Ahead

As electric mobility continues to evolve,silence will remain the new luxury. But luxury isn’t just about what youdon’thear, it’s about how motion feels, how components respond, and how seamlessly every interaction happens.

With the right damping solutions, we can make sure that in tomorrow’s vehicles,comfort won’t just be heard, it will be felt.

Discover the AutoDamp™ Seriesif you’re an OEM or Tier-1 supplier looking to elevate the in-cabin experience, our experts can help you choose or develop the right damping solution for your application.

BLOG

Enhancing Reliability and Efficiency in the Textile Industry with Specialty Lubricants

3 min read | 25 September 2025

The textile industry is highly process-oriented, with each application posing its own set of challenges. From high-speed machinery to extreme operating temperatures and humid environments, every stage of production demands lubrication solutions that are not only reliable but also efficient in the long run. At Molygraph, we specialize in providing tailor-made lubrication solutions that address these exact challenges, helping textile units minimize downtime, reduce costs, and achieve smoother operations.

The Challenge in Textile Operations

One of the most critical equipment in textile processing is the stenter machine, which operates under extreme conditions. The continuous chain traverses through hot chambers with operating temperatures reaching up to +200°C. Conventional chain oils often fail in such environments, leading to:

High oil consumption due to rapid evaporation.

Frequent re-lubrication needs.

Hard carbon deposits on chains.

Energy losses and reduced operational efficiency.

These challenges translate into higher operating costs and frequent interruptions in production.

The Molygraph Solution: STENOL 200 ST (N)

To address these issues, Molygraph introducedSTENOL 200 ST (N) – High-performance stenter chain oil, specifically formulated for the demanding conditions of stenter machines.

Key Features:

Keeps chains clean with low residue and minimal carbonization.

Prevents oil dripping, even at high operating temperatures.

Ensures prolonged lubrication and smoother chain movement.

Reduces wear & tear, extending equipment life.

Suitable for both domestic and imported stenter machines, as well as polyfilm stretching chains, STENOL 200 ST(N)  provides consistent performance even under the harshest conditions.

Real-World Success: Western India Textile House

A leading textile house in western India faced high lubrication costs and frequent re-lubrication in its stenter machines. After switching toour product, they achieved:

20% reduction in chain oil expenses.

Improved machine efficiencywith faster speeds and reduced energy consumption.

Significant reduction in carbon deposits,resulting in smoother machine operation.

Reliable supply supportthrough Molygraph’s strong local distribution network.

Why Partner with Molygraph?

Beyond products, Molygraph offers a consultative approach—working with clients to analyze operations, recommend the right solutions, and even conduct trials to calculate ROI. With more than three decades of expertise, our solutions are engineered not just for lubrication but forefficiency, reliability, and sustainability.

Looking to improve your textile plant’s efficiency and reduce lubrication costs? Get in touch with our experts today atsales@molygraph.com

pages.text:
BLOG

Choosing the Right High-Speed Grease for Your Application

5 min read | 20 December 2024

High-speed bearings are critical components in many industrial applications. Unlike standard processing equipment, these bearings rotate at significantly higher speeds, demanding specialized lubricants to ensure optimal performance and longevity. Selecting the right grease is essential to prevent excessive heat generation, drag, and premature failure.

Understanding the Challenges of High-Speed Applications

During site visits, one of the most common questions I encounter is, “Why do high-speed bearings run hotter?” The answer lies in the interaction between the bearing's speed and the lubricant used. Bearings that rotate at high speeds generate considerable friction, and using an unsuitable grease can exacerbate the issue, leading to excessive heat and reduced lifespan.

For instance, I once examined an overhanging fan in an industrial facility. The fan, belt-driven by a motor running at 1,750 RPM, used a grease that was too thick for its application. The mismatch caused overheating and decreased the bearing's service life. This highlights the importance of selecting a grease designed for high-speed operations.

Calculating the Speed Factor

The speed factor determines the relationship between a bearing's rotational speed and size, guiding grease selection.

DN Value: Multiply the inner diameter of the bearing by its speed.

NDm Value: Multiply the median bearing size (pitch diameter) by its rotational speed.

These values help pinpoint the required grease properties, including oil viscosity and National Lubricating Grease Institute (NLGI) grade.

Key Considerations for High-Speed Grease Selection

Choosing the right grease involves understanding several factors that influence its performance:

1. Base Oil Viscosity

The viscosity of the grease’s base oil must match the operational needs of the bearing. For high-speed bearings, a lower viscosity is typically required to reduce friction and heat generation.

2. Channeling Characteristics

Greases with good channeling properties flow easily to fill voids, minimizing churning and heat buildup. Barium complex grease’s balance of flowability and stability ensures efficient lubrication.

3. Dropping Point

A high dropping point indicates the grease's ability to withstand elevated temperatures without breaking down. Barium complex grease exceeds the typical requirements for high-speed bearings, offering a significant safety margin.

4. Thickener Type

The thickener determines the grease's overall consistency and mechanical behavior. The barium-complex thickener used in this grease provides superior thermal and mechanical stability, critical for high-speed applications.

5. NLGI Grade

The grease’s consistency, measured by its NLGI grade, must strike a balance between ease of application and sufficient viscosity for effective lubrication.

6. Additives

Additives enhance the performance of the grease by providing additional load-bearing capacity, oxidation resistance, and wear protection.

Practical Application and Monitoring

While these properties guide grease selection, field trials are essential to confirm the product's performance in real-world conditions. During the trial, monitor bearing temperature, grease leakage, and overall equipment performance to ensure compatibility.

Additionally, ensure proper cleaning of old grease to prevent compatibility issues when switching to a new product. This is particularly important when introducing barium complex grease into your operations.

A Step-by-Step Guide for Selecting High-Speed Grease

Determine Speed Factor: Use the DN or NDm value to calculate the bearing’s speed.

Evaluate Operating Temperature: Ensure the grease can handle the maximum temperature with a suitable margin.

Check Compatibility: Assess the compatibility of thickeners if mixing greases is unavoidable.

Field Test: Monitor bearing temperature, grease retention, and overall performance during a trial phase.

pages.title: https://www.molygraph.com/newsroom/the-truth-behind-grease-colors--what-you-need-to-know-for-optimal-lubrication
pages.text:
BLOG

The Truth Behind Grease Colors: What You Need to Know for Optimal Lubrication

8 min read | 05 November 2024

When it comes to lubricating grease, a wide variety of colors flood the market—black, red, blue, white, and even green. These colors can be visually appealing, but do they actually affect the performance of grease? While many industries have long associated certain colors with specific functions, the color itself often has little impact on the essential properties of lubricating grease. In this blog, we’ll uncover the real story behind grease colors and what factors truly matter in ensuring the right grease for your application.

A Brief History of Grease and Its Evolving Use

Lubricating grease has been around for centuries, with its history dating back to ancient Egypt. For a long time, grease was evaluated by physical characteristics like feel, odor, and appearance. This was a rudimentary method, as little was known about the actual science behind lubrication until the discovery of crude oil in the 19th century. Fast forward to the 20th and 21st centuries, where significant progress has been made in grease technology. Today, we use sophisticated tests to measure vital properties such as consistency, mechanical stability, load-carrying capacity, and resistance to water and oxidation—none of which are linked to color.

Why Is Grease Colored?

There are many reasons for adding color to grease, from aesthetic appeal to functional differentiation. For example, white greases are often associated with food-grade machinery, black greases contain molybdenum or graphite for heavy-duty applications, and red greases are sometimes linked to high-temperature use. However, these color associations are not absolute, and relying on color alone can lead to misjudgments when selecting the right grease for a particular application.

Key takeaway: The color of grease is more of a visual cue and doesn’t typically influence its performance.

Do Grease Colors Impact Lubrication Performance?

Although many users think that color hints at a grease’s performance, studies show otherwise. Below, we explore the main factors affecting lubricating grease performance, none of which are dependent on color:

Consistency and Mechanical Stability: Grease consistency determines how well it stays in place during operation, while mechanical stability indicates its ability to resist breakdown. These are vital in ensuring proper lubrication under different loads, speeds, and operating conditions. The type of thickener used in grease plays a major role in these factors—not the color.

Load-Carrying Capacity and Wear Resistance: Additives, such as extreme pressure (EP) agents, determine how much load a grease can carry before it breaks down. Certain greases include solid lubricants like molybdenum disulfide (often giving them a black color), but the color itself is not what enhances load-carrying capacity or wear resistance. It’s the additive chemistry.

Water Resistance and Oxidation Stability: Water ingress and oxidation can rapidly degrade grease. Calcium sulfonate and aluminum complex greases are often preferred for high water resistance. In these cases, the color has no bearing on the grease’s ability to repel water or resist oxidation.

The Effects of Additives and Dyes on Grease Color

While the color of grease doesn’t directly influence performance, the additives used to achieve certain colors can sometimes have an indirect effect. For example, white greases often contain titanium dioxide (TiO2) or zinc oxide (ZnO), which can slightly alter some properties. When used in higher concentrations (over 5%), these whiteners can soften the grease’s penetration or increase oil separation in some lithium and aluminum complex greases.

Similarly, dyes are used to achieve bright colors such as red or blue. Although dyes are typically added in minimal quantities (0.001-0.5%), using them in higher concentrations (above 0.1%) can negatively affect certain properties like copper corrosion resistance.

Key takeaway: While additives used to color grease may slightly alter its properties, the color itself is not the defining factor in performance.

How Environmental Factors Impact Grease Color

Grease color can also change over time due to environmental exposure. Factors such as sunlight, heat, and water can fade or darken grease color without affecting its functional properties. For example, red grease exposed to water might change to an orange hue but will still perform its intended lubrication tasks. Heat exposure can darken greases, but this color change does not indicate a loss of lubrication quality.

Key takeaway: Color changes due to environmental factors don’t necessarily affect the grease’s performance.

The Importance of Choosing the Right Grease for Your Application

When selecting grease, it’s crucial to focus on the technical specifications rather than just its appearance. Consider the following when evaluating lubricating grease:

Consistency: Ensure that the grease has the right NLGI grade for your application.

Additives: Check for anti-wear, extreme pressure, or anti-corrosion additives that suit your operational conditions.

Base oil viscosity: Higher viscosity is best for slow-speed, high-load applications, while lower viscosity is ideal for high-speed applications.

Water resistance: If your machinery operates in wet conditions, choose a grease with excellent water-repelling properties.

Don’t Judge Grease by Its Color

Although colored greases have become a mainstay in the industry, the color itself should not be the deciding factor when choosing a lubricating grease. Instead, focus on the performance properties that matter—consistency, stability, load-carrying capacity, and water resistance. These are the true indicators of a grease’s quality and longevity in service.

Ultimately, understanding how grease works and what properties matter most will ensure that your machines remain well-lubricated and in optimal condition, regardless of the color.

Contact Us

Share

Certain greases include solid lubricants like molybdenum disulfide (often giving them a black color), but the color itself is not what enhances load-carrying capacity or wear resistance. It’s the additive chemistry.

Water Resistance and Oxidation Stability: Water ingress and oxidation can rapidly degrade grease. Calcium sulfonate and aluminum complex greases are often preferred for high water resistance. In these cases, the color has no bearing on the grease’s ability to repel water or resist oxidation.

The Effects of Additives and Dyes on Grease Color

While the color of grease doesn’t directly influence performance, the additives used to achieve certain colors can sometimes have an indirect effect. For example, white greases often contain titanium dioxide (TiO2) or zinc oxide (ZnO), which can slightly alter some properties. When used in higher concentrations (over 5%), these whiteners can soften the grease’s penetration or increase oil separation in some lithium and aluminum complex greases.

Similarly, dyes are used to achieve bright colors such as red or blue. Although dyes are typically added in minimal quantities (0.001-0.5%), using them in higher concentrations (above 0.1%) can negatively affect certain properties like copper corrosion resistance.

Key takeaway: While additives used to color grease may slightly alter its properties, the color itself is not the defining factor in performance.

How Environmental Factors Impact Grease Color

Grease color can also change over time due to environmental exposure. Factors such as sunlight, heat, and water can fade or darken grease color without affecting its functional properties. For example, red grease exposed to water might change to an orange hue but will still perform its intended lubrication tasks. Heat exposure can darken greases, but this color change does not indicate a loss of lubrication quality.

Key takeaway: Color changes due to environmental factors don’t necessarily affect the grease’s performance.

The Importance of Choosing the Right Grease for Your Application

When selecting grease, it’s crucial to focus on the technical specifications rather than just its appearance. Consider the following when evaluating lubricating grease:

Consistency: Ensure that the grease has the right NLGI grade for your application.

Additives: Check for anti-wear, extreme pressure, or anti-corrosion additives that suit your operational conditions.

Base oil viscosity: Higher viscosity is best for slow-speed, high-load applications, while lower viscosity is ideal for high-speed applications.

Water resistance: If your machinery operates in wet conditions, choose a grease with excellent water-repelling properties.

Don’t Judge Grease by Its Color

Although colored greases have become a mainstay in the industry, the color itself should not be the deciding factor when choosing a lubricating grease. Instead, focus on the performance properties that matter—consistency, stability, load-carrying capacity, and water resistance. These are the true indicators of a grease’s quality and longevity in service.

Ultimately, understanding how grease works and what properties matter most will ensure that your machines remain well-lubricated and in optimal condition, regardless of the color.

BLOG

Power of Silicone Grease - The Ultimate Solution for Lubrication and Protection

4 min read | 03 Jan 2025

Silicone grease is a versatile, high-performance lubricant widely recognized for its durability, resistance to extreme temperatures, and compatibility with a wide range of materials. Whether you are sealing O-rings, ensuring electrical insulation, or enhancing the performance of industrial components, silicone grease offers unmatched efficiency and reliability.

Our HVG Series of silicone greases is designed to cater to diverse applications across industries. Here’s a closer look at the benefits of silicone grease and how our HVG product range can meet your needs.

Key Benefits of Silicone Grease

Temperature Resistance

Silicone grease remains stable across extreme temperatures, making it ideal for both high-heat and freezing environments.

Dielectric Properties

It is an excellent electrical insulator, providing protection against electrical shocks and preventing flashovers.

Chemical Inertness

Silicone grease resists chemical degradation, ensuring longevity even in harsh environments.

Non-Melting Composition

Unlike many other greases, silicone grease retains its form, making it perfect for high-vacuum and pressure systems.

Compatibility

It works seamlessly with most plastics, rubbers, and elastomers without causing damage or deterioration.

Introducing the HVG Series: Customized for Every Application

1. HVG 14

A high-performance dielectric compound.

Benefits: Excellent dielectric properties, anti-tracking coating, and prevention of electrical shocks.

Applications: Ideal for spark plug wires, automotive wiring connectors, and battery terminals.

2. HVG 21

A high-vacuum, non-melt silicone grease.

Benefits: Exceptional chemical resistance and sealability for vacuum pressures up to 10⁻⁶ torr.

Applications: Perfect for O-rings, gaskets, seals, plastics, PVC, and shut-off valves.

3. HVG 28/1

A soft-grade multipurpose silicone grease.

Benefits: Non-toxic, compatible with plastics and elastomers, and ideal for hot and cold water systems.

Applications: Used in faucets, valves, and laboratory equipment such as glass taps and desiccators.

4. HVG 28/6

A highly adhesive transparent silicone grease.

Benefits: Tacky, non-melting, and non-toxic; does not affect plastic or rubber parts.

Applications: Designed for chemical industry valves, sanitary fittings, and rubber and plastic components.

5. HVG 7

A silicone-based mold release agent.

Benefits: Ensures easy mold release, provides a smooth surface finish, and protects against moisture and fumes.

Applications: Ideal for complex molds in HDPE water tanks, plastic, and rubber parts.

Choosing the Right Silicone Grease for Your Needs

For electrical insulation, choose HVG 14.

For vacuum systems, opt for HVG 21.

For multi-purpose lubrication, HVG 28/1 and HVG 28/6 are excellent choices.

For mold release applications, HVG 7 delivers outstanding performance.

Why Molygraph’s HVG Series?

At Molygraph, we are committed to providing high-performance lubrication solutions that enhance efficiency and reliability. Our HVG silicone grease series offers unmatched versatility, ensuring your equipment and components perform at their best.

Whether you’re in the automotive, industrial, or consumer sectors, silicone grease is a must for efficient and long-lasting lubrication.

Get in touch with us today to learn more about our HVG range and find the perfect solution for your applications -https://www.molygraph.com/products?productName=HVG&page=1

BLOG

End-to-End Lubrication Solutions for the Steel Industry

7 min read | 26 Feb 2026

Steel production is not just the backbone of modern infrastructure; it's also one of the most demanding industrial processes, where every step involves high mechanical loads, extreme temperatures, contamination, and constant pressure to optimize output while minimizing downtime.

In such a high-stress environment, lubrication plays a critical role. It’s not merely about keeping machines running; it’s about protecting assets, improving safety, enhancing energy efficiency, and ultimately driving profitability. From the intense heat of rotary kilns in sponge iron units to the precise requirements of continuous casting machines and the high-speed demands of rolling mills, the choice of lubricant can influence everything from component longevity to operational stability.

The Role of Lubrication Across the Steel Value Chain

Each stage of steel manufacturing presents its own unique set of challenges. A one-size-fits-all approach doesn’t work. The lubrication strategy needs to be nuanced, tailored, and responsive to both the application and its environment.

1. Sponge Iron & Pelletization

Rotary kilns and slip seal applications demand greases that can endure extreme temperatures and abrasive environments. Here, high-viscosity, wear-resistant greases are essential to prevent component degradation and gear failure. Advanced formulations provide robust protective films, reducing wear and extending re-lubrication intervals.

2. Sinter Plants & Blast Furnaces

Open gears, rotary mixers, and skip cars operate under intermittent loads and temperature shocks. In these areas, graphite-enriched greases and aluminium complex thickeners offer the dual benefits of thermal stability and shock load resistance. Equipment like hydraulic mud guns and drill chains also benefits from high-load greases that remain pumpable across varying ambient conditions.

3. Continuous Casting Machines (CCM)

Water ingress and high loads are the primary challenges here. The greases used must be water-resistant, shear-stable, and capable of maintaining consistency under constant stress. Bearings in withdrawal sections, turrets, and roller tables require long-life EP (Extreme Pressure) greases with enhanced corrosion protection.

4. Hot & Cold Rolling Mills

The rolling stage demands precision and resilience. Work roll bearings, chokes, and drive shafts undergo continuous stress at high speeds and elevated temperatures. Calcium sulfonate and polyurea greases have become reliable solutions for these areas due to their mechanical stability, water washout resistance, and load-handling capabilities.

5. Auxiliary Systems and Finishing Lines

Beyond the core processes, support equipment such as cooling beds, cranes, fans, re-heating furnaces, and pickling lines requires greases suited to intermittent operation, chemical exposure, or fluctuating temperatures. Multipurpose greases that combine anti-wear, anti-corrosion, and thermal stability properties serve these needs well.

Lubricants for Real-World Demands

Today’s lubricants must do more than reduce friction. They must reduce maintenance frequency, optimize energy consumption, resist contamination, and ensure machine availability even under unpredictable conditions. This demands an in-depth understanding of tribology, metallurgy, and plant operations.

Lubricant manufacturers working closely with steel plants are now offering:

Tailored solutionsbased on component-level assessments

Field-tested performance dataunder actual plant conditions

Support for centralized lubrication systemsand automated grease lines

Problem-solving partnershipsbeyond the product — extending to training, audits, and monitoring

Lubrication and the Shift Towards Sustainable Steelmaking

As the global steel industry moves toward decarbonization and cleaner production, lubricants are also evolving. There is a growing shift toward biodegradable and low-toxicity formulations that meet performance standards without compromising environmental responsibility. Greases with longer service life not only reduce waste but also lower overall lubricant consumption, helping plants meet both economic and ESG goals.

Eco-conscious lubrication strategies now focus on:

Minimizing lubricant leakage and runoff

Using formulations with renewable or non-toxic base oils

Enhancing energy efficiency through friction reduction

Lowering emissions associated with frequent lubricant replacement

Molygraph’s Role in This Landscape

With over 50 years of experience in specialty lubrication, Molygraph has been a technical ally for the steel sector, not just as a supplier but as a collaborator. We offer an expansive range of products, from open gear greases and slip seal lubricants to long-life bearing greases and high-performance sprayable lubricants, all backed by rigorous testing and expert field support.

OurSpraylube Series,Ultramill Greases, andSGH serieshave become industry standards across multiple steel facilities. But more importantly, our application engineers work on-site with plants to fine-tune lubrication schedules, conduct root-cause failure analyses, and drive cost-saving maintenance strategies.

Lubrication may not be the most visible element in a steel plant, but its impact is undeniable. It determines whether a machine runs efficiently or breaks down prematurely. It affects not just costs, but safety, uptime, and sustainability. As the industry evolves, the need for smart, adaptable lubrication strategies becomes more important than ever.

Share

BLOG

The Potential of Chain Oils: Why Right Lubricant Matters?

7 min read | 25 Feb 2025

Chain drives are an indispensable part of industrial operations, offering a reliable solution for transmitting power, moving materials, and operating machinery. Chains, comprising individual links with rollers, bushings, and interconnecting links, endure significant stress and wear over time. The proper lubrication of these chains is crucial—not only for their longevity but also for the overall efficiency and reliability of the equipment they drive.

This article explores the importance of chain oils, the challenges they address, and how Molygraph’s Stenol series and Safol Chain oil series offer cutting-edge solutions for diverse industrial needs.

Why Chain Oils Are Essential

Lubricating a chain does more than reduce friction; it ensures the durability of both the chain and its accompanying sprockets. Here are the key benefits of effective chain lubrication:

Rust Protection: A protective oil film prevents corrosion and extends the life of the chain.

Wear Minimization: Proper lubrication reduces friction between rollers and bushings, which are critical areas of wear.

Optimized Performance: Reduces operational noise, energy consumption, and maintenance requirements.

Challenges in Chain Lubrication

Chains often operate in harsh environments, which can compromise their performance if not properly lubricated. Common challenges include:

High Temperatures: Many chains operate in environments with extreme heat, such as bakeries ovens or industrial ovens.

Dust and Water Spray: Contaminants can degrade chain performance and increase wear.

Need for Penetration: Lubricants must effectively reach inner friction points, such as the areas between rollers and bushings.

Drip-Free Applications: Avoiding excess lubricant dripping is critical in many industries, especially food processing.

What should you look for in Chain oils?

One of the critical factors to consider when choosing chain oils is their evaporation rate. Low evaporation rates are essential to ensure the lubricant stays on the chain for longer durations, reducing the need for frequent reapplication. When the oil evaporates it is important that it is a clean burning and leaves minimum residue behind. The nature of the residue can significantly impact chain performance. A well-formulated chain oil strikes the perfect balance, offering excellent thermal stability, cleanability and leaving a functional layer to maintain smooth operation.

Molygraph’s Chain Oil Solutions

Safol Chain Oil 220 HT

This NSF-certified, HALAL, and KOSHER-certified synthetic oil is a versatile solution for high-temperature chains, ensuring superior performance even under challenging conditions.

Key Benefits

NSF H1 certified for use in the food processing industry.

Excellent penetration to inner chain components.

Long service life with exceptional anti-wear protection.

Synthetic formulation minimizes residue formation and carbonization, keeping chains clean.

Application Areas

Conveyor and drive chains.

High-temperature applications such as bakeries.

Safol Chain Oil 320 H1

This is an NSF NSF-certified high-temperature Universal Food Grade Chain Oil.

Key Benefits

NSF H1 Registered For The Food Processing Industry.

Provides Wear Protection.

Designed Primarily For The Effective.

Lubrication Of All Types Of High-Temperature Chains.

Operate Under High Temperature And Extended Service Intervals.

Application Areas

For High-temperature Bakery Chains.

Suitable For Automatic Lubricators.

For All Types Of High-temperature Chains And Conveyors.

STENOL Premium 220

This is a premium grade high-performance high-temperature universal chain oil.

Key Benefits

Being synthetic oil the chain remains clean due to low residue formation and carbonization.

Long service life of chains due to outstanding anti-wear protection.

Longer wet film life with lower deposit and varnish formation.

Outstanding extreme pressure performance.

Application Areas

Chain drive control and conveyor chains at high temperatures.

Conveyor Chains operating under heavy loads and high temperatures.

Slide ways, open bearings, drying and varnishing plants, stenters, and powder coating machines.

STENOL Premium 300 W

Key Benefits

Improved penetration and better wetting of the friction points.

Good adhesion and sufficient carry-over in long machines.

Being synthetic oil the chain remains clean due to low residue formation and carbonization.

Low evaporation rate at high temperature.

No varnish build-up over the chain surface.

Long service life of chains due to outstanding anti-wear protection hence reducing chain drag at high temperatures.

Hydrocarbon base oil for extremely low-temperature applications.

Application Areas

Conveyor Chains operating under heavy loads and high temperature - Wood Industry.

Can be used in continuous belt presses like MDF, HDF, OSB, or LVL.

Used to lubricate roller bar chains that are exposed to radiant heat.

Additionally, it can be used for Stentor frame / Glass / Ceramic ovens also.

Why Choose Molygraph?

Molygraph’s synthetic chain oils are designed to meet the diverse needs of industries ranging from food processing to manufacturing. With a focus on reliability, safety, and performance, our products ensure:

Enhanced machine uptime.

Compliance with global safety and quality standards.

Reduced maintenance costs due to superior lubrication and protection.

Investing in the right chain oil is more than a maintenance decision; it’s a strategic move to boost productivity, extend equipment life, and ensure seamless operations.

Explore our range of high-performance lubricants today.
